chunks.page_summary: This article provides guidelines for retrofitting VAF ViscoSense viscosity and density sensors with Rheonics SRV inline viscometers or SRD inline density and viscosity meters.
product_names: SRD - Inline Density and Viscosity meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Rheonics SRV and SRD - VAF ViscoSense Retrofit
Modified on: Wed, 26 Feb, 2025 at 9:16 AM
What is the purpose of this article?
To provide guidelines on how the Rheonics SRV inline viscometer or SRD density and viscosity meter can retrofit the VAF ViscoSense line of viscosity and density sensors.
What products are involved?
Rheonics SRV (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) or SR V - Inline Viscosity Meter (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) and SRD - Inline Density and Viscosity meter (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/).
1. Introduction
Rheonics sensors are used in multiple industrial applications to measure the viscosity and/or density of fluids, enabling highly accurate tracking and tight control of the fluid.
The Rheonics SRV (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) sensor measures viscosity and temperature inline, while the Rheonics SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/) sensor measures density in addition to viscosity and temperature.
The sensor probes are compact, light, hermetically sealed, and factory-calibrated, so they don't need re-calibration during its operational lifetime.
Rheonics sensors use a balanced torsional resonator (BTR) as sensing technology, which vibrates torsionally and is connected to the outside world at a node. The BTR technology patented by Rheonics offers a significant advantage over competitors in the market, as it allows the SR-sensors to be compact, lightweight, and resistant to external vibrations. Review Rheonics sensor operating principle (https://rheonics.com/whitepapers/).
2. Retrofit
Users may need to replace a viscometer or density meter in line due to malfunction, lack of spare parts, or to simply try out Rheonics sensors and the BTR technology advantages.
In those cases, the user most likely has an existing installation point from the former sensor that he may want to reuse to install the Rheonics Type-SR sensor. The SRV and SRD favor retrofit installations due to the modular design of the probe. Rheonics offers different probe variants, these are flush, short and long insertion sensors, with all process connections available while ensuring consistency of performance across all variants.
What needs to be verified for good installation of the SRV and SRD?
The SRV and SRD probes have a sensing area that should be free of obstructions and deposits. These areas should be immersed in the fluid of interest. Review this article for more information: Installation Guidelines for inline process SRV viscometer and SRD density and viscosity meter
key_facts: The article provides guidelines for retrofitting VAF ViscoSense sensors with Rheonics SRV inline viscometers or SRD inline density and viscosity meters.
Rheonics SRV sensors measure viscosity and temperature, while Rheonics SRD sensors measure density, viscosity, and temperature.
Rheonics sensor probes are compact, light, hermetically sealed, and factory-calibrated, eliminating the need for re-calibration during their operational lifetime.
Rheonics sensors utilize a patented Balanced Torsional Resonator (BTR) technology, which provides advantages such as compactness, lightweight design, and resistance to external vibrations.
The modular design of Rheonics SRV and SRD probes facilitates retrofit installations.
Rheonics offers various probe variants including flush, short, and long insertion sensors, compatible with all process connections.
For proper installation, the sensing area of SRV and SRD probes must be free of obstructions and deposits and fully immersed in the fluid of interest.
warnings_notes: For good installation of SRV and SRD probes, the sensing area should be free of obstructions and deposits and fully immersed in the fluid of interest.

chunks.page_summary: This page details Rheonics SRV sensor versatility for retrofitting existing installations and provides a compatibility overview with VAF Instruments' ViscoSense viscometers and density sensors.
chunks.extracted_text:
https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000411886-installation-guidelines-for-inline-viscometer-srv-and-density-meter-srd

By selecting the correct insertion length “A” and process connection “B”, Rheonics Type-SR sensors can be ordered to retrofit almost all existing installations, while having the sensing area correctly placed. Figure 1 shows the SRV-X4 (Flush Tri-Clamp), -X2 (Short threaded) and -X5 (Long insertion flanged), all with customizable process connection.

Rheonics favors open standards and these also provide a way for customer to order an alternative sensor when they plan to replace Rheonics ones.

Figure 1: Rheonics SRV sensor versatility on design.

3. VAF Instruments

VAF Instruments (https://www.vaf.nl/) is a company mainly focused on the maritime industry and builts sensors and solutions specially suited for that area.

VAF ViscoSense:

The ViscoSense, viscosity and density meters, is one of VAF product lines for inline measurement and process control of ship’s fuel to achieve combustion efficiency. The ViscoSense3 is a viscosity and temperature sensor and the ViscoSense3D adds density as a third reading. Rheonics offers the SRV and SRD sensors for potential retrofits of the VAF units.

Table 1: Rheonics and VAF Instrument viscometer and density meters

VAF Instrument | Rheonics sensor for retrofit
ViscoSense 3 | Rheonics viscometer SRV
ViscoSense 3D | Rheonics density and viscosity meter SRD

Rheonics SRV Viscometer
70 [2.76]
124 [4.87]

VAF ViscoSense 3
77.3 [3.05]
169 [6.64]
key_facts: Rheonics Type-SR sensors can be customized with specific insertion lengths ("A") and process connections ("B") to retrofit existing installations.
Rheonics Type-SR sensors mentioned include SRV-X4 (Flush Tri-Clamp), SRV-X2 (Short threaded), and SRV-X5 (Long insertion flanged).
Rheonics promotes open standards, allowing customers to use alternative sensors if they wish to replace Rheonics ones.
VAF Instruments is a company focused on providing sensors and solutions for the maritime industry.
The VAF ViscoSense product line includes viscosity and density meters for inline measurement and process control of ship's fuel.
The VAF ViscoSense3 is a sensor that measures viscosity and temperature.
The VAF ViscoSense3D is an advanced sensor that measures viscosity, temperature, and density.
Rheonics SRV and SRD sensors are compatible and offered as retrofit options for VAF ViscoSense units.
diagram_labels: Figure 1: Rheonics SRV sensor versatility on design.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an installation overview and a detailed dimensional drawing for Rheonics VAF ViscoSense sensors in a ductile iron housing, including various flange specifications and associated part numbers.

Installation of the VAF ViscoSense sensors require a ductile iron sensor housing specially designed for marine applications. The ViscoSense housing varies in sizes and it is shown in Figure 3 and 4. The sensor probe is threaded in the housing which has flanges as connection to the process.

Figure 4: ViscoSense sensor and housing detailed [1].

Rheonics sensors don't need the use of a factory designed housing for operation, but the sensor can be ordered to retrofit the standard VAF housing if this housing is to be reused.
See Figure 5 as an example. The Rheonics SRV viscometer can be ordered to replace a VAF probe and use the existing housing. Depending on the housing size and fluid type, the required SRV probe for VAF retrofit is a short probe with custom threaded connection (SRV-X1-XXX). For specific cases, where long insertion is required, -a slimline long insertion probe with custom connection (SRV-X6) can be used.

152.4 [6.00]

Figure 5: Rheonics SRV viscometer retrofit for VAF ViscoSense - mm [in]. Download drawing.
(https://rheonics.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Rheonics-Viscometer-SRV-VAF-Retrofit.pdf)
key_facts: Rheonics sensors can retrofit into standard VAF housings, eliminating the need for a factory-designed housing.
The Rheonics SRV viscometer can replace a VAF probe in existing VAF housings.
For VAF retrofit, a short SRV probe with custom threaded connection (SRV-X1-XXX) is available.
For specific cases requiring long insertion, a slimline long insertion probe with custom connection (SRV-X6) can be used.
The ViscoSense V2 sensor, HEX SET SCREW, and FLOW TUBE are made from AISI 316L.
The O-RING used in the ViscoSense V2 sensor assembly is made of VITON and has an ID of 25.12 mm and a thickness of 1.78 mm.
The ViscoSense V2 sensor and housing assembly is rated for a maximum pressure of 16 Bar and a maximum temperature of 180 °C.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides technical drawings and descriptions for retrofitting Rheonics density and viscosity meters (SRD and Type-SR) into existing VAF ViscoSense and Viscotherm housings, including detailed dimensions.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 6: Rheonics SRD density and viscosity meter retrofit for VAF ViscoSense - mm [in]. Download drawing.

VAF ViscoSense in Viscotherm housing

Viscotherm is an an older viscosity measuring system from VAF. The Viscotherm housing (Figure 6) now enables users to retrofit the original Viscotherm system entirely. Once again, Rheonics Type-SR sensors can be mounted in these housings with the correct configuration as in Figure 7.

Figure 7: Viscosense on Viscotherm housing (VAF order code: 0401-0816) [2].
key_facts: Rheonics SRD density and viscosity meters can be retrofitted into VAF ViscoSense housings.
Viscotherm is an older viscosity measuring system manufactured by VAF.
The Viscotherm housing (as referenced in Figure 6, and specifically shown in Figure 7) allows for the complete retrofit of the original Viscotherm system.
Rheonics Type-SR sensors can be mounted in these Viscotherm housings with the configuration shown in Figure 7.
The VAF order code for the Viscotherm housing shown is 0401-0816.
diagram_labels: Rheonics Density and Viscosity meter SRD
warnings_notes: Reference [2] is mentioned in Figure 7's caption but not defined on this page.

4. How to order a Rheonics Type-SR sensor for retrofit?
To get a quote for VAF viscosense, do the following:
a. Use the online RFQ pages for SRV inline viscometer (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srv/) and SRD viscosity and density meter (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srd/).
b. Select the operating conditions (temperature and pressure rating) and sensor specifications (electronics, measurement range, calibration, etc.)
c. For process connections, select X1 - custom thread. Use the fillable box below the process connection options to write "VAF retrofit required". Specify the thread type required, if different to M40x1.5.
d. If possible, send installation pictures or drawings of the sensor to be replaced to info@rheonics.com
key_facts: Rheonics provides retrofit solutions for VAF ViscoSense using their SRV viscometer and SRD density and viscosity meter.
Dimensional drawings for both SRV viscometer and SRD density and viscosity meter retrofits for VAF ViscoSense are available for download in PDF format.
To obtain a quote for a VAF ViscoSense retrofit, customers should use the online RFQ pages for SRV inline viscometers or SRD viscosity and density meters.
Ordering requires specifying operating conditions (temperature, pressure rating) and sensor specifications (electronics, measurement range, calibration).
For process connections, customers must select 'X1 - custom thread' and specify 'VAF retrofit required' in the provided box.
The default custom thread type is M40x1.5, but other thread types can be specified.
It is recommended to send installation pictures or drawings of the sensor being replaced to info@rheonics.com for retrofit orders.
diagram_labels: Rheonics density and viscosity meter SRD

chunks.page_summary: This page provides important notes regarding Rheonics sensors for retrofits, a disclaimer about document illustrations, external references, and a list of related articles concerning Rheonics SRV and SRD products.
product_names: SRV X6 Slimline Long Insertion probe
chunks.extracted_text:
Note: Rheonics sensors are suitable for retrofits but care should be taken to comply with minimum installation requirements for the sensing element to ensure reproducible and repeatable readings. These can be reviewed in any other article for SRV and SRD (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000297658 and https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000297659).

Contact the technical sales team at info@rheonics.com or Technical support team at support@rheonics.com if needed to help you get the correct retrofit.
